jobs.title: Senior QA Automation Engineer
jobs.description: About Juniper Square Our mission is to unlock the full potential of private markets. Privately owned assets like commercial real estate, private equity, and venture capital make up half of our financial ecosystem yet remain inaccessible to most people. We are digitizing these markets, and as a result, bringing efficiency, transparency, and access to one of the most productive corners of our financial ecosystem. If you care about making the world a better place by making markets work better through technology – all while contributing as a member of a values-driven organization – we want to hear from you. Juniper Square offers employees a variety of ways to work, ranging from a fully remote experience to working full-time in one of our physical offices. We invest heavily in digital-first operations, allowing our teams to collaborate effectively across 27 U.S. states, 2 Canadian Provinces, India, Luxembourg, and England. We also have physical offices in San Francisco, New York City, Mumbai and Bangalore for employees who prefer to work in an office some or all of the time. About your role As a QA Engineer at Juniper Square, you will collaborate with the product and engineering teams to help define and drive our manual and automation testing efforts. You must be detail oriented, passionate about product quality, and a strong advocate for the end user experience. What you’ll do Review functional specifications and technical design documents, providing meaningful feedback and using AI-assisted analysis to identify edge cases or logic gaps early in the SDLC. Work closely with development teams to identify test cases for automation and leverage AI-powered IDEs (like Cursor or Augment) to rapidly prototype and scaffold new test suites. Design, develop, and execute test cases against product features and backend systems. Maintain and extend automation frameworks (Playwright/Locust) by utilizing LLMs for code refactoring, optimizing test scripts, and reducing flaky test patterns. Contribute to your development team on best practices, processes, and problem-solving, including the ethical and effective use of AI tools in the QA workflow. Develop automated test result reports and highlight any potential quality risks Identify, troubleshoot, and track bugs to resolution, using AI tools to assist in root cause analysis and log interpretation. Be an advocate for automation: Partner with the engineering team to provide recommendations on how to decrease manual efforts through automated solutions and AI-driven test generation. Experience designing evaluation frameworks for LLM-powered features, including prompt regression testing and behavioral drift detection Proactively leverage AI tools (e.g., Cursor, Claude, etc…) to accelerate test authoring, debugging, and maintenance of automation frameworks Use AI to diagnose failures, generate test scenarios, and improve coverage and efficiency Contribute to testing strategies for AI-powered features, including validation

Representing more than 40% of global GDP, these industries are the infrastructure of our planet, including agriculture, construction, field services, transportation, and manufacturing — and we are excited to help digitally transform their operations at scale. We set up our employees for success and have built a culture that encourages rapid career development, countless opportunities to experiment and master your craft in a hyper-growth environment. You're energized by our opportunity: The vision we have to digitize large sectors of the global economy requires your full focus and best efforts to bring forth creative, ambitious ideas for our customers. This is a hands-on automation-heavy role working on the Summit platform — a core banking system that manages financial products (bonds, loans, payments, transactions) and interfaces with accounting and core banking systems. About the Role: You will support two key workstreams: Maintaining and enhancing testing for core banking applications, including upgrades, defect resolution, and supporting project changes. Improving and optimizing nightly batch processing for performance and reliability. This is a great opportunity for an experienced automation engineer who wants to make an immediate impact in a stable financial institution environment. Location: Hybrid in Chicago – 3 days onsite preferred. Strong remote candidates will also be considered. Duration: 6 months contract with strong likelihood of extension Start: ASAP / Early June Must-Have Qualifications: 5–7+ years of Software Development Engineer in Test (SDET) or Test Automation experience Strong C# and Selenium expertise Proven ability to build automation frameworks from scratch Postman experience for API/backend testing (this role is heavily backend-focused) Recent experience working in banking or a financial institution Heavy automation focus (70%+ automation vs manual testing) Key Technical Skills: Selenium WebDriver with C# Postman / API testing Test framework design and architecture Azure DevOps (for test case management and defect tracking) SQL and understanding of batch processes Agile / SDLC knowledge What We’re Looking For (Soft Skills): Proactive with a “bias for action” and ability to hit the ground running Curious learner who can work independently Strong communicator and collaborative team player Ownership mindset – you take initiative and drive improvements Interview Process: Round 1: 30-minute Teams call with Hiring Manager Final Round: 1-hour technical interview with the team (virtual or in-person welcome) Beacon Hill is an Equal Opportunity Employer that values the strength diversity brings to the workplace. jobs.title: Test Automation Engineer Lead
jobs.description: Symetra has an exciting new opportunity to join us as a Test Automation Engineer Lead! About The Role The Test Automation Engineer Lead is a hands‑on technical leader responsible for guiding a small team of 2–3 automation engineers while driving the development of scalable, high‑quality test automation frameworks. This role works with minimal oversight and collaborates closely with business and technical partners to ensure reliable, high‑performing system delivery and to advance automation maturity across the organization. What You Will Do Lead a team of automation engineers in designing, developing, and maintaining automated test strategies, scripts, and frameworks across functional, regression, API, UI, and integration testing; recommend test environments, frameworks, and tool stacks. Mentor and coach automation engineers, analysts, and interns; serve as the automation subject‑matter expert across teams. Drive automation strategy, including solution planning, framework design, CI/CD integration, and tooling decisions for key initiatives. Champion and enforce automated testing best practices across teams. Troubleshoot and resolve complex automation defects and framework issues; lead root‑cause analysis to prevent recurring failures. Own success metrics and end‑to‑end accountability for automation frameworks and CI/CD pipelines, including communicating automation health and risk to leadership. Identify and implement process improvements to enhance automation efficiency, reliability, and maintainability. Collaborate with business and technical SMEs, development teams, QA resources, and external vendors; communicate regularly with senior IT and cross‑functional stakeholders. Why Work at Symetra Here’s what some of our employees have to say about why they work at Symetra: "Symetra will spoil you. There aren't many companies that will put as much effort into you being a success as Symetra. The culture will challenge you but simultaneously put you at ease. Failure is seen as integral to the learning process so there is less reason to be fearful of it." - Kerry S., Sr. Systems Analyst “If you want to invest in yourself and build a career, this is a great place to work. If you don't want to put in the effort to learn the industry and become a student of the business (it takes real effort!), there are easier places to work.” - Cris H., VP Internal Sales What We Offer You Benefits and Perks We don’t take a “one-size-fits-all” approach when it comes to our employees. Our programs are designed to make your life better both at work and at home. Flexible full-time or hybrid telecommuting arrangements Plan for your future with our 401(k) plan and take advantage of immediate vesting and company matching up to 6% Paid time away including vacation and sick time, flex days and ten paid holidays Give back to your community and double your impact through our company matching Want more details? Check out our Symetra Benefits Overview Compensation Salary Range: $128,7

Our modules allow sites to capture data electronically in real-time, providing data managers and monitors with enhanced transparency, and enabling remote monitoring by sponsors and CROs. The ideal candidate is a proactive and analytical tester with strong manual testing expertise and a passion for delivering high-quality software in a regulated healthcare environment. This role emphasizes deep functional validation, exploratory testing, and end-to-end quality ownership. You will play a critical role in designing and executing comprehensive test strategies, identifying edge cases, and ensuring product reliability across the software development lifecycle. Exploratory Testing: Conduct in-depth exploratory testing to uncover edge cases, usability issues, and gaps not covered by predefined test cases. Regression Testing: Own and execute manual regression test suites to ensure stability of existing functionality, with a focus on high-risk and business-critical areas. jobs.description: This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a QA Automation Engineer in United States. This role offers the opportunity to join a dynamic product development environment focused on building and enhancing a large-scale web application for customer support. You will work at the intersection of engineering, product, and design, contributing directly to the quality and reliability of digital services used by thousands of users. The position combines hands-on test automation with manual and exploratory testing, ensuring robust delivery across web and mobile platforms. You will play a key role in defining testing strategies, improving automation coverage, and strengthening overall QA practices within an Agile team. The environment is collaborative, innovation-driven, and centered on continuous improvement, with strong emphasis on ownership and high-quality engineering standards. Accountabilities Work closely with cross-functional teams including product managers, developers, designers, and analysts to ensure high-quality delivery of digital solutions. Design, plan, execute, and maintain comprehensive test strategies, test cases, and test scenarios across functional and regression testing cycles. Perform manual testing including acceptance and regression testing to validate product functionality and user experience. Develop, maintain, and enhance automated test scripts and frameworks to improve testing efficiency and coverage. Conduct API testing and integration testing, ensuring system reliability across services and components. Prepare detailed test reports, documentation, and quality metrics to support decision-making and release readiness. Contribute to continuous improvement of QA processes, tools, and best practices within an Agile/Scrum environment. Requirements 2+ years of experience in QA automation and software testing roles within Agile teams. Strong hands-on experience with automation tools such as Selenium and programming skills in Java. Experience with API testing tools such as SOAP UI and performance testing tools like JMeter. Solid understanding of web and mobile UI manual testing principles and test design techniques. Good knowledge of RESTful APIs and experience in testing and automating API validations. Basic understanding of SQL and web technologies including HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Experience working with Atlassian tools such as Jira and Confluence. Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and problem-solving abilities. Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively in cross-functional teams. Benefits Flexible work setup: remote or office-based, with workplace arrangement support. Remote onboarding for a smooth start from anywhere. Performance-based bonuses (annual or quarterly depending on role). Access to internal learning platforms, knowledge libraries, and partner training programs. Health and life insurance coverage. jobs.description: VetsEZ is seeking a mid-level Test Automation Engineer to support a large federal healthcare technology program. This product line manages various software products, middleware platforms, system integrations, health data integrations, and Electronic Health Records (EHR) integrations. The ideal candidate will bring strong experience in test automation, API testing, and enterprise integration testing within Agile and DevSecOps environments. The candidate must reside within the continental US. Responsibilities Develop and maintain automated test solutions in collaboration with software developers, analysts, and functional testers. Design and execute automated testing strategies across middleware, API, and data integration layers. Build and maintain scalable Selenium-based automation frameworks using Java and TestNG. Perform API automation testing using tools such as RestAssured, Karate, ReadyAPI, and SoapUI. Execute functional, regression, integration, performance, and data validation testing activities. Collaborate with QA leads, developers, and business stakeholders to troubleshoot issues and improve testing efficiency. Support CI/CD pipeline automation and testing activities within Agile and DevSecOps environments. Utilize tools such as JIRA, SQL, AWS services, and automation frameworks to support testing efforts. Stay current on emerging technologies and testing best practices. Take on additional tasks and responsibilities as needed to support team objectives and ensure the success of the project. Requirements Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical discipline. Minimum of 10 years of experience in software testing and quality assurance. Strong hands-on experience with Selenium automation frameworks using Java and TestNG. Recent experience writing, debugging, and maintaining Java-based automation scripts. Experience with API automation testing using RestAssured, Karate, ReadyAPI, or SoapUI. Experience testing middleware, REST APIs, backend services, and AWS-based applications. Strong understanding of CI/CD, Agile, and DevSecOps testing practices. Experience with SQL/data validation and tools such as JIRA and Section 508 testing tools. Strong communication, troubleshooting, and collaboration skills. Additional Qualifications Experience supporting the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA), healthcare systems, or EHR integrations preferred. Familiarity with AWS services such as Lambda, Step Functions, CloudWatch, and related technologies is a plus. Ability to obtain and maintain a government clearance. Benefits Medical/Dental/Vision 401k with Employer Match PTO + Federal Holidays Corporate Laptop Training opportunities Remote Opportunity Note: Selected candidates will be required to complete fingerprinting at a government facility and undergo a background check as part of the hiring process. VetsEZ is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race,
